projects.name: Flying Tulip

crowdsales.lockupPeriod: 100% at TGE, with tokens initially wrapped in the Perpetual PUT structure.

projects.shortDescription: Flying Tulip is an on-chain exchange that provides spot trading, perpetual contracts, lending, options, and structured yield.

icoData.description:
January 29, 2026 - Flying Tulip has raised an additional $25.5 million in a private token Series A round, bringing total institutional capital raised to $225.5 million.

September 29, 2025 - Andre Cronje's crypto project Flying Tulip has raised $200 million with a token valuation of $1 billion and aims to create an on-chain exchange. 

About Flying Tulip public sale. Details: 

  • Token Price & Total Value: $0.10 each. The full project value starts at $1 billion (based on 10 billion total tokens).
  • 100% Safety Net: Every buyer gets a special "PUT option" (PUT NFT) that protects your full investment. If the token price drops, you can always get your original money back. 
  • No Limits on Supply, But It Only Shrinks: Max 10 billion FT tokens ever. No new ones added – only burned (destroyed).
  • No vesting periods; tokens are yours immediately.
  • Fundraising metod: Use stablecoins (like USDC/USDT), staked ETH (stETH), staked SOL (jupSOL), AVAX, or sUSDe. Your money goes into safe, on-chain yields (no risky loans or bridges) to earn interest. That interest funds the project first, then buys back and burns FT tokens to boost value.
  • Hold: users can hold tokens and keep their protection NFT PUT option.
  • Withdraw Tokens: users can take their FT (lose protection forever) and sell them on markets. 
  • Important Note: Only direct sale buyers get the protection. If you buy FT later on exchanges, no safety net.

projects.name: Lighter
projects.shortDescription: Lighter is a perpetual trading protocol based on Ethereum.

icoData.description: November 11, 2025 - Lighter announced had raised $68 million in a new funding round. The round was led by Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund and fintech investor Ribbit Capital, with participation from Haun Ventures and the online brokerage Robinhood, which rarely makes venture investments. The funding valued Lighter at around $1.5 billion, according to sources familiar with the deal. Founded in 2022 by 40-year-old CEO Novakovski, Lighter completed the raise through a combination of equity and token warrants tied to a yet-to-be-released cryptocurrency.
